fields.description:
# Tier-1 opportunistic detached drain + `rebar enrich` CLI

## Why
The feature must run async across server/PC/Mac/cloud with ZERO client setup â€” no daemon, broker, or scheduler. The proven pattern (git gc --auto, npm update-notifier) piggybacks a cheap, detached drain on ordinary invocations. rebar ships the POSIX half of this already for async push (`src/rebar/_store/push.py:108`).

## What
- A `maybe_drain()` gate on ordinary invocations: a cheap "is anything soaked+unclaimed?" check that no-ops in the common case, else DETACHES the enrichment to a child that outlives the command.
- **Detach, cross-platform (DERISKED at API level):** POSIX reuses push.py's approach (`start_new_session=True`, `close_fds=True`) + its PYTHONPATH bootstrap. push.py has NO Windows path â€” it calls `start_new_session=True` unconditionally, which on Windows maps to `CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP` and does NOT orphan-protect. Windows is therefore NEW work, branched on `os.name` (the established codebase pattern; cf. `acli_subprocess.py`): `creationflags=subprocess.DETACHED_PROCESS | subprocess.CREATE_NO_WINDOW` (these constants exist only on Windows â€” reference them ONLY inside the `os.name == "nt"` branch or the POSIX import AttributeErrors).
- **Observability:** the child configures a `logging.handlers.RotatingFileHandler` (maxBytes+backupCount bounded) writing to `.rebar/logs/enrich-drain.log` (git-ignored) and redirects its own stderr there; stdout/stdin are DEVNULL â€” drain failures stay diagnosable.
- **Drainer loop (owns DONE + self-healing + growth-bound, moved here from S3):** from `pending_enrichment(now)` PLUS the fallback scan (tickets certified but with absent/stale digest â€” S3's defense-in-depth), claim one (read-reduce-check-append under `lock.acquire`, per S3), run `enrich` (S1), write `TICKET_DIGEST` (S2), append `DONE_ENRICH`, then `prune` the ticket's queue events (keep the latest cycle) so log growth is bounded. Process UP TO `DRAIN_BATCH` per run, then exit. Per-item error handling: an `enrich` exception (LLM timeout/rate-limit) releases the claim (lease expiry) and is logged; the batch continues to the next ticket; the failed ticket is re-picked on a later drain.
- **CLI `rebar enrich`:** `--drain` (bounded drain, the detached target), `--once` (claim+process one entry, in-process, tests), `status` (prints JSON `{"pending":int,"claimed":int,"soaking":int}` from the queue reducer). `overlap_drain âˆˆ {off, async, always}`, DEFAULT `async` (`async` = the opportunistic detached drain of this design; `always` = synchronous inline drain for tests/CI; `off` = no drain). Opt-out is `overlap_drain = "off"` on `LLMConfig` (`[tool.rebar.llm]`), env `REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_DRAIN` (the ONE canonical name â€” used in What, Scope, and ACs identically). `overlap_drain` is a STRING enum (`off|async|always`) so it lives on `LLMConfig` (read via `from_env`), NOT `VerifyConfig` (which is bool-only via `_as_bool`).

## Constants (defaults stated)
`DRAIN_BATCH = 5`, `DRAIN_GATE_BUDGET_MS = 20` â€” both on `LLMConfig`.

## maybe_drain injection point (decided â€” no "or")
Async push is actually triggered inside `src/rebar/_store/event_append.py` (`push.push_tickets_branch`, line ~189), so `maybe_drain()` rides the SAME opportunistic seam: called once at the end of the write-command path in `event_append`, immediately after the push, behind the cheap soaked?-check (the check no-ops on the common event so it does not fire per-event-append meaningfully). Single authoritative site; NOT `review_plan`/`claim` individually.

## Dependencies
S1 (enrich op), S2 (digest store), S3 (queue + claim primitive). This story WIRES them; they are prerequisite sibling stories, not existing code.

## Scope
- New `src/rebar/llm/enrich_drain.py`; CLI arm in `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py`; the `maybe_drain` hook in `src/rebar/_store/event_append.py` (beside `push_tickets_branch`); config fields `overlap_drain`/`overlap_drain_batch`/`overlap_drain_gate_budget_ms` on `LLMConfig` (`src/rebar/llm/config.py`).
- OUT: native scheduler (idea Tier-2), CI drain (idea Tier-3).

## Testing
- `tests/unit/test_enrich_drain.py`: `test_once` (one entry end-to-end, FakeRunner); `test_batch_cap` (`DRAIN_BATCH` bound, backlog drains over runs); `test_gate_latency` (maybe_drain no-op < `DRAIN_GATE_BUDGET_MS`, no spawn when nothing soaked); `test_lock_held_skip` (silent exit 0, logged); `test_enrich_error_continues` (one enrich() raises â†’ claim released, batch continues); `test_no_key_noop` ([agents]/key absent â†’ clean no-op); `test_opt_out` (`REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_DRAIN=off`); `test_self_heal` (certified-but-stale-digest ticket picked up by fallback scan); `test_windows_detach_kwargs` (os.name branch selects creationflags â€” mocked).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `rebar enrich --drain` processes up to `DRAIN_BATCH` (default 5) soaked+unclaimed entries then exits; backlog drains over successive runs; `pytest ...::test_batch_cap`.
- [ ] `maybe_drain` no-ops with no spawn when nothing soaked, within `DRAIN_GATE_BUDGET_MS` (20 ms); detaches a surviving child otherwise; `::{test_gate_latency}`.
- [ ] Concurrent drains don't double-process (lock-held â†’ silent exit 0, logged to `.rebar/logs/enrich-drain.log`); `::test_lock_held_skip`.
- [ ] A single `enrich` failure releases the claim, logs, and the batch continues; the ticket re-drains later; `::test_enrich_error_continues`. Self-healing fallback picks up cert-without-fresh-digest tickets; `::test_self_heal`.
- [ ] `[agents]`/key absent â†’ clean no-op; `REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_DRAIN=off` disables the opportunistic drain (single canonical env name); `::{test_no_key_noop,test_opt_out}`.
- [ ] Detach is `os.name`-branched: POSIX `start_new_session`, Windows `DETACHED_PROCESS|CREATE_NO_WINDOW` (referenced only in the nt branch); `::test_windows_detach_kwargs`. Each processed ticket gets a `DONE_ENRICH` append + a `prune` of its queue events (bounding growth); verified by `::test_batch_cap` observing DONE + a post-drain pending count of 0.
